J1.basis: the legacy blog's email-cleanup topics won't carry into the new positioning ... start building non-brand content/targeting toward meeting-notes intent (currently zero footprint in GSC)
J1.rationale: Clearly recognizes the pivot — live site rewritten as AI meeting assistant, email-cleanup treated as legacy, meeting-notes as the new (currently unranked) target — but the pivot is asserted in passing rather than framed up front as the plan's organizing reality, and it never contrasts the live product against the GSC history explicitly.

J2.basis: Rebuild /blog/[slug] route + /api/posts ... so the 12 already-ranking post URLs serve real content again
J2.rationale: Treats the 12 posts as a single undifferentiated block to restore wholesale — no per-asset disposition, no harvest/redirect/sunset choices, and not a single impression or position figure from the GSC export is cited to justify the blanket 'serve them all again' call.

J3.basis: those URLs already carry real search demand that's currently being thrown away ... the legacy blog's email-cleanup topics won't carry into the new positioning
J3.rationale: Avoids both extremes in spirit — it preserves legacy URL equity (restore the ranking pages) while pushing new meeting-notes content — but it never names the tension, doesn't sequence the transition, and doesn't reconcile restoring inbox-cleanup pages with its own claim that those topics won't carry, leaving the middle path asserted rather than grounded.

J1.basis: start building non-brand content/targeting toward meeting-notes intent (currently zero footprint in GSC) — the legacy blog's email-cleanup topics won't carry into the new positioning

J2.rationale: Treats all 12 posts as one undifferentiated block to restore, with no per-asset harvest/redirect/sunset disposition and zero citation of actual impressions/positions from the export — essentially a blanket 'keep/restore everything.'

J3.rationale: Gestures at both sides (restore legacy equity in #3, build meeting-notes cluster in #6) but never articulates the tension or a bridging/redirect plan, and the two items contradict — restoring email-cleanup content while conceding it won't carry is closer to muddled than a sequenced transition.

J1.basis: start building non-brand content/targeting toward meeting-notes intent (currently zero footprint in GSC) — the legacy blog's email-cleanup topics won't carry into the new positioning
J1.rationale: The entrant clearly recognizes the pivot — it rewrites metadata to the AI meeting assistant and explicitly notes the email-cleanup legacy topics won't carry forward — but it never lays out the three-source picture (live site vs. legacy GSC vs. stale repo) as a stated framing, keeping the inference implicit.

J2.basis: Rebuild `/blog/[slug]` route + `/api/posts` ... so the 12 already-ranking post URLs serve real content again
J2.rationale: The 12 posts are treated as one undifferentiated block to 'serve real content again' with no per-asset harvest/redirect/sunset disposition and no citation of actual impressions or positions from the GSC export — just a blanket 'restore them all' instruction.

J3.basis: those URLs already carry real search demand that's currently being thrown away ... the legacy blog's email-cleanup topics won't carry into the new positioning
J3.rationale: It avoids both extremes — preserving the ranking URLs' equity while pushing a new meeting-notes cluster — but the tension is never articulated and the plan is internally muddy (restore all 12 inbox-era posts yet declare their topics irrelevant), with no real sequencing of the transition.
